summary: The diff implements most of the requested redesign requirements (REQ-1 through REQ-6) comprehensively. REQ-7 references to the two generated image files are present in the component code (Home.tsx uses hero-blush.dim_1920x1080.jpg, Book.tsx uses book-banner.dim_1920x600.jpg), but the actual binary image files themselves do not appear as added files in the diff. Additionally, frontend/index.css (the root-level CSS file) is not shown as modified, only frontend/src/index.css. One minor concern is that tailwind.config.js still uses hsl() wrappers for several tokens (card, popover, primary, etc.) while the CSS custom properties are defined in OKLCH, which could cause token mismatch issues.

missing_requirements.requirement: Generate and render a new hero background image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-blush.dim_1920x1080.jpg and a book banner at frontend/public/assets/generated/book-banner.dim_1920x600.jpg as actual static asset files.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows 0 added files. The image files themselves are not present in the added files list. While the components reference the correct paths, the actual image assets are not included in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Update CSS custom properties in both frontend/src/index.css AND frontend/index.css to reflect new tokens consistently across light and dark modes.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff only shows frontend/src/index.css being modified. The root-level frontend/index.css is not listed among modified files, leaving it potentially out of sync with the new design tokens.

hallucinated_features.description: Tailwind config uses hsl() color function wrappers for card, popover, primary, secondary, muted, accent, destructive, border, input, and ring tokens while CSS custom properties are defined in OKLCH format, creating a functional mismatch.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request asked for Tailwind custom colors to match the new OKLCH CSS custom property values, but the tailwind.config.js wraps many tokens in hsl() instead of oklch(), which would break those color mappings. This was not a requested behavior.
